password_reset_poison.description: Host header injection in password reset -- attacker-controlled Host value included in reset link sent via email

probes.description: Override Host header to attacker domain on password reset endpoint -- if reset email contains attacker host, token is leaked
X-Forwarded-Host override on password reset -- some frameworks prefer X-Forwarded-Host for link generation

routing_abuse.description: Host header routing abuse -- manipulate internal routing to access restricted virtual hosts or admin panels

probes.description: Host header set to localhost -- may route to admin vhost or internal application
Host header set to internal hostname -- may bypass reverse proxy ACLs

ssrf_via_host.description: SSRF via Host header -- backend uses Host header value to make internal requests

probes.description: Absolute URL with overridden Host -- send GET http://target/path with Host: internal to reach internal services

cache_poison_via_host.description: Web cache poisoning via Host header -- inject malicious host into cached responses if Host is unkeyed

probes.description: Inject evil host and check if reflected in response with cache headers -- indicates cache uses Host for content but not for key

duplicate_host.description: Duplicate Host header -- send two Host headers to exploit parsing differences between proxy and backend

probes.description: Two Host headers -- proxy uses first (legitimate), backend uses second (attacker-controlled) for link generation
